rules.action: Avoid adding risk in bearish regimes; optionally trim risk and raise cash.
rules.evidence: Supported by tactical allocation and trend-following research.
rules.formula: trend_state = bullish if close > SMA(close, 200), bearish otherwise
rules.name: 200-day trend regime filter
rules.rationale: Long-term trend rules reduce the chance of staying fully exposed during major bear markets.

rules.action: Trim hyped/overextended exposure; consider washed-out candidates only with trend confirmation.
rules.evidence: Commonly used in Bollinger-style band analysis; best used as confirmation, not a standalone signal.
rules.name: Volatility-adjusted extension bands
rules.rationale: Standard deviation bands express whether a move is unusual relative to recent volatility.

rules.action: Trim overweight assets and add to underweight assets when rules allow.
rules.evidence: Vanguard frames rebalancing primarily as a risk-control discipline.
rules.formula: drift = current_weight - target_weight; rebalance when abs(drift) > threshold
rules.name: Threshold rebalancing
rules.rationale: Keeps portfolio risk aligned with the intended plan and reduces emotional decision-making.

rules.name: Single-position cap
rules.rationale: Prevents one mistake or one winner from becoming a portfolio-level concentration risk.

rules.action: Trim sector members proportionally when the sector exceeds cap.
rules.evidence: Diversification principle; implemented as a quantitative portfolio constraint.
rules.name: Sector concentration cap
rules.rationale: Avoids hidden correlation risk when many holdings depend on the same macro/sector driver.

rules.action: Block adds to losing positions unless there is a documented thesis reset.
rules.evidence: Behavioral finance guardrail consistent with RIA/Lance Roberts risk rules.
rules.name: No automatic averaging down
rules.rationale: Prevents hope-based averaging down from masquerading as disciplined investing.

rules.action: Partially trim when drawdown breaches configured threshold.
rules.evidence: Risk-management principle emphasized by RIA and drawdown mathematics.
rules.name: Entry-price stop risk
rules.rationale: Large losses require disproportionately large gains to recover.

rules.action: Partially trim when trailing drawdown breaches threshold.
rules.name: Trailing high drawdown control
rules.rationale: Protects gains and prevents strong winners from becoming large round trips.

rules.action: Prefer adds to positive-momentum assets with strong relative ranking.
rules.evidence: Partially implemented as signal feature; strategy harness still pending.
rules.name: Relative and absolute momentum
rules.rationale: Momentum has long-running academic evidence across equities and asset classes.

rules.action: Use high valuation as a risk-context modifier, not as a precise timing signal.
rules.formula: valuation_regime = percentile(CAPE or valuation proxy over long history)
rules.name: Valuation awareness
rules.rationale: Valuation metrics have long-horizon return information but weak short-term timing power.

rules.action: Reduce risky exposure when realized volatility materially exceeds target.
rules.evidence: Useful for comparison harness; not yet active in recommendations.
rules.formula: scale = target_vol / realized_vol, bounded by min/max exposure
rules.name: Volatility targeting
rules.rationale: Keeps risk budget more stable across changing volatility regimes.

rules.action: Block adds or trim when predicted next-horizon drawdown risk is high.
rules.name: ML drawdown-risk overlay
rules.rationale: A transparent ML overlay can help rank risk, but must remain explainable and subordinate to rules.
